lyrics:
In my dreams I would fly
And each morning remember
Where I had gone
And how I got there

I thought and I flew
Free from time and space
An effortless journey
The freedom of anyplace

“You know dreams aren’t real,” I said
And I never flew again

But that was then, when now seemed far away
The line between the boy I was and who I am today
Looking back down the hall
Empty frames on fading walls
It was never meant to end
But that was then

Fell asleep to the radio
Woke singing songs I’d never heard
I couldn’t put the guitar down
Wrote lyrics I thought would matter

I was certain, absolutely
My songs would ‘change the world’
But kids and love and music
Became a weight I could not hold

And finally, one sleepless dawn
I let the music go

But that was then, when now seemed far away
The line between the choice I made and who I am today
Looking back down the hall
Empty frames on fading walls
It was never meant to end
But that was then

“Maybe you’re just getting old”
She dismissed my fears
So, I pressed ahead
Ignored them, as they returned

I cannot keep pretending
This is nothing when it isn’t
Countless tests only showed
What it wasn’t

And then I knew what it had always been…
And everything was different

But that was then, when now seemed far away
The line between the man I was and what I am today
Looking back down the hall
Empty frames on fading walls
I was never meant to end
But that was then

But that was then, when now seemed far away
The line between my whole life and who I am today
Looking back down the hall
Empty frames on fading walls
I was always meant to end
I wish I’d known this then

impression.text: This track opens with a warm, reflective texture centered around acoustic guitar and a clean, melodic electric guitar, creating a nostalgic and earnest atmosphere. The energy is dynamic, building from gentle, storyteller verses into swelling, full-band choruses that feel both powerful and heartfelt. The production is clean and spacious, with a well-defined low end and a clear lead vocal that sits comfortably in the center of the mix. A defining quality is the powerful emotional lift into the chorus, where the drums kick in with a more driving feel, perfectly underscoring the lyrical theme of looking back in time.

review.review_text: Rose builds 'But That Was Then' through three distinct losses: the childhood dream of flight, a music career let go at dawn, and finally something heavier, a diagnosis that reframes everything before it. The lyric that turns the whole song is the last chorus shift, 'I was always meant to end / I wish I'd known this then,' and Rose earns it because the verses have done the patient, honest work of stacking regret without self-pity. In G major and at a tempo that feels brisk on paper but lands as purposeful in the room, the arrangement pulls the classic move of stripping back for verses and opening up for choruses, but the production has enough space in the mix that it never feels mechanical; the clean electric guitar line threading through the first section has the same clear-eyed warmth you get from early John Hiatt, and the drums kicking in at the chorus provide exactly the lift the lyric needs. At nearly six minutes this asks for your full attention, and it rewards it.
anchors_used: lyric: 'I was always meant to end / I wish I'd known this then'
tempo framing: 143.6 BPM reads purposeful rather than driven
structural strip-back in verses vs. full-band choruses
clean electric guitar line in opening section
